tldr: Headlines worldwide report in near-identical terms that PSG defender and Morocco World Cup captain Achraf Hakimi will stand trial for rape after a French appeals court confirmed the referral, while Brazil's feeds in this cluster carry entirely unrelated domestic politics and TV programming.

frames.sub_frame: rape trial confirmed against PSG star

evidence.quote: Hakimi to be tried in France for rape
Achraf Hakimi sera jugé pour viol
to stand trial on rape charge
to stand trial for alleged rape

Hakimi to stand trial on rape charge

French court confirms Morocco and PSG player

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline across the corpus to frame the case as an interruption of a brilliant career — 'Brillant avec le Maroc et le PSG, Achraf Hakimi rattrapé par un procès pour viol' — the verb rattrapé (caught up by) carries an editorial narrative of glory overtaken by scandal, absent from every other factual title.

Sole headline to lead with 'Morocco's World Cup captain' as the primary identifier, foregrounding his active tournament role over club affiliation or nationality — a framing that emphasises present-tense stakes no other outlet chose.

bottom_line: The headline layer speaks with exceptional uniformity — a footballer faces a confirmed rape trial — while Brazil's bucket contributes only unrelated domestic content, producing the cluster's sharpest thematic silence.
